learning_outcomes.description: To provide students with a broad art historical and stylistic overview of Euroamerican modernism.
To familiarise students with both the important artworks and critical arguments of the period.
To encourage students to engage with the artworks and ideas that came out of modernism.
To develop students ability to respond verbally to works of art.
To develop students skills in writing and critical thinking.

Students will complete the worksheets during the tutorials of each week.
assessments.task_description: Weekly worksheets to be completed by hand, in tutorials: completion of weekly worksheets during tutorial times. Worksheets will include 1-2 questions that relate directly to the set reading/s for the week. There will be 10 worksheets over the semester. Each worksheet is worth 3 points. Total points achievable: 30 points.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Completed by hand and submitted to tutor in tutorials.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submissions will not be accepted unless alternative arrangements have been discussed with the tutor, supported by relevant documentation (eg. medical certificate) to explain absence from tutorials and an alternative arrangement has been approved.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Students will be required to write a short formal academic essay of 1000 words based on a question. You will be given three questions to choose from: select ONE of the questions to write your short essay. These questions are all based on the required course readings and lectures content from week 1 to week 6 included. Please note that this essay is different from a research essay. Students will NOT need to consult texts beyond the ones listed in the course reading list. Word count is 1000 words. Footnotes are not included in the word count.

The essay must include references to the set readings, formatted correctly in Chicago Manual of Style (Notes and Bibliography Style). Please check the following link for more info about referencing rules: Chicago-Style Citation Quick Guide

The assessment will be marked against these evenly weighted criteria:

  • Ability to reference the readings and lectures content;
  • Visual analysis: ability to reference visual objects to support your argument;
  • Structure: coherent essay structure that prioritises key points;
  • Expression and formatting: high standard of written expression
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using TurnItIn. Before submitting any assignments for this course you must ensure you have completed UQ's compulsory online Academic Integrity Tutorial.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Inspera Assessment will be used in ARTT1105.

This assessment is an invigilated, on-campus digital exam, which students will complete using their own device (or a loaned device from the UQ Library). Refer to Inspera Assessment which includes an introduction to Inspera functionality and where to go for support. Further information will be provided on the Blackboard site for this course in Week 1 of the semester, and practice opportunities will be available throughout the semester.

A 120 minute Inspera examination comprising short essay questions about course content including set readings and lectures.

There will be 4 short essay questions to answer; each question is worth 10 marks each. The questions will test your comprehension of the major ideas and debates covered in the course, as well as your ability to think critically about them and to convey your arguments clearly through the use of correct grammar, spelling, and syntax.

 The assessment will be marked against these evenly weighted criteria:

  • Ability to reference the readings and lectures content;
  • Visual analysis: ability to reference visual objects to support your argument;
  • Structure: coherent essay structure that prioritises key points;
  • Expression and formatting: high standard of written expression
assessments.submission_guidelines: UQ is presently rolling out a new assessment tool, Inspera Assessment. Inspera Assessment provides additional functionality to complement the existing suite of digital learning tools at UQ with a greater range of question types and accessibility provisions.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
